tipRules.tip: Palaniuk: 'Balsa crankbaits have a great action for the spring because they are not super aggressive. Fish them on transitions and first breaks when bass are sitting in that 4-6 foot range.'

Palaniuk: 'A squarebill-style bait with a wider front end that tapers toward the back has been deadly around rock transitions. If you find any laydown or wood still in the water, it is almost a guarantee you will catch one.'

Palaniuk: 'Since it's a lot clearer than normal, this year has been even better for a jerkbait. Any time the water level is moving up or down, some fish tend to pull off the bank and suspend. That is where the jerkbait is so good.'

knowledge.insight: Palaniuk: 'When water is still really low and clearer than normal in the spring, fish want to go shallow but there is nothing for them to get on -- they are just sitting and waiting on those transitions, the first breaks. Shallow crankbaits become the top producer.'

Palaniuk: 'When the water hits the upper 40s and low 50s, fish start getting more active and willing to eat a big bait. Northern strain bass get much more aggressive sooner in the year since they are used to living in a colder climate.'
Palaniuk: 'I fish the same areas that I throw a crankbait or jerkbait, but use the glide bait when I am after the really big ones.'
